summary: Stunning matrix recording with crystal‑clear “Roses” and a high‑energy set highlighted by a killer China > Rider jam, soaring Sugar Magnolia and superb Jerry Garcia vocals.
review: The matrix blend of board and audience delivers exceptional clarity and a near‑surround sound that makes this August 1982 show feel like you’re right on the stage.  Performances are praised as “on” throughout, with standout moments including an extended China > Rider sequence, a fiery Eyes of the World, and a rocking Sugar Magnolia, while one reviewer notes the setlist felt a bit sloppy.  Overall the recording quality is top‑tier and the musical flow is largely exhilarating.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give five‑star praise for both the performance and the matrix sound, citing excellent clarity and standout jams; only a single moderate comment calls the show sloppy, yielding an overall strong but slightly tempered rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Matrix blend of board and audience provides remarkable depth and near‑surround clarity; a few reviewers note minor channel glitches but these do not detract from overall superb sound.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Long, upbeat first set (≈80 min) followed by a massive second set with extended jams; despite a “crazy” setlist some feel the show was sloppy, but most describe the pacing as high‑energy and well‑structured.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Voice described as cool, clear and crisp; guitar work noted as extremely on point, hitting every note with incredible phasing.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass highlighted for dropping huge bombs during Sugar Magnolia.
